metadata.notes: agent=yes daemon=full driver=gap:wait_turn ‚Äî agy retries after a failing command within one continuous working span; failing function_call_output sets IsError but is non-settling, so no flicker. wait_turn is a driver stub.

assessment.body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: full
driver_capability: gap:wait_turn

## Reasoning

The scenario asserts a SINGLE contiguous `working` span across retry cycles: a failing tool result, then a corrected retry, with NO flicker to `ready` or `waiting` between the failed attempt and the recovery, ending `ready`.

### agent_supports: yes

This is the BEST-fit antigravity scenario ‚Äî the real samples capture exactly this self-correction loop. In `~/.gemini/antigravity-cli/brain/9ea5200c-...`, the agent runs `bd ready --json` (RUN_COMMAND result: "The command failed with exit code: 1"), inspects the failure, retries with `git status` ("failed with exit code: 128"), then keeps iterating on corrected commands ‚Äî all inside one turn. The longer `69343e3e-...` sample does 17+ run_command cycles with interleaved failures and successes, self-correcting each time, before the terminal turn_done. agy genuinely runs the run‚Üífail‚Üíinspect‚Üíretry loop.

### daemon_capability: full

Walking the no-flicker invariant against `parser.go`:

1. **`ready ‚Üí working`** ‚Äî `parseUserInput` ‚Üí `user_message` on the opening prompt.
2. **Each attempt** ‚Äî `parsePlannerResponse` WITH tool_calls ‚Üí `assistant_message` + synthesized ToolUse (non-settling, stays `working`).
3. **Failing result** ‚Äî `parseToolResult` ‚Üí `function_call_output` with `IsError = true` (the classifier `commandFailed` matches the result text "The command failed"; parser.go ~L143-152, ~L172-174). Critically, `function_call_output` is explicitly a NON-SETTLING event (docstring: "so the turn stays working until the terminal PLANNER_RESPONSE"), and `IsError` does NOT settle the state ‚Äî there is no error‚Üíready or error‚Üíwaiting path. So a failed attempt keeps the session `working`, no flicker.
4. **Retry** ‚Äî the next `assistant_message`+ToolUse and its result repeat, all within the same continuous `working` span.
5. **`working ‚Üí ready`** ‚Äî only the terminal empty `MODEL/PLANNER_RESPONSE` ‚Üí `turn_done` ‚Üí `ready`.

The antigravity parser has no intermediate settle and no waiting path, so the spec's hard invariant ("state stays continuously working across all retry cycles; no flicker") holds structurally ‚Üí `daemon=full`. The scenario's `ready ‚Üí working ‚Üí ready` topology with ‚â•1 tool call and final `ready` matches.

### driver_capability: gap:wait_turn

The recipe needs `send` + `wait_turn` + `sleep`; `wait_turn()` is a driver stub (DRIVE_ELICITS="send slash sleep"). The retry loop terminates on the terminal empty PLANNER_RESPONSE; wait_turn must poll for it (and must NOT return on an intermediate failing result). `record` ports the wait_turn seam + launch_repl session-resolution SEAM before driving. INTERACTIVE recipe.
caveats: Tokens/cost not in the antigravity JSONL; observations assert tokens_nonzero:false, cost_nonzero:false. Not a daemon bug.

No-flicker invariant is structural: function_call_output (incl. IsError) is a non-settling event in the antigravity parser, and there is no waiting path ‚Äî only the terminal empty PLANNER_RESPONSE settles to ready. The failing-result IsError classifier is unit-tested (TestParseTurn / TestSuccessfulResultNotError).

Strongly grounded: the live sample 9ea5200c shows bd ready‚Üífail‚Üígit status‚Üífail‚Üíretry, and 69343e3e shows 17+ self-correcting run_command cycles in one turn. record promotes provisional‚Üísettled.
driver=gap:wait_turn: wait_turn is a driver stub (must not return on a mid-turn failing result); record ports it + the launch_repl session-resolution SEAM first.

sources.note: parseToolResult ‚Üí function_call_output with IsError when commandFailed matches 'The command failed' (~L143-152, ~L172-174); function_call_output is explicitly NON-settling (turn stays working); no error/waiting settle path ‚Äî only terminal empty PLANNER_RESPONSE ‚Üí turn_done ‚Üí ready.

Real sample: run_command `bd ready` ‚Üí RUN_COMMAND 'failed with exit code: 1' ‚Üí retry git status ‚Üí 'failed with exit code: 128' ‚Üí continues self-correcting ‚Äî exactly the scenario's retry loop, all in one turn.
TestParseTurn asserts a failing RUN_COMMAND result ‚Üí function_call_output with IsError and closes the open tool; TestSuccessfulResultNotError guards the classifier ‚Äî neither settles the turn.
DRIVE_ELICITS="send slash sleep"; wait_turn() is a stub ‚Üí driver=gap:wait_turn.

script.text: Run the shell command `false` (it will fail). Inspect the failure, then run `echo recovered` to recover. Work through this autonomously without asking me anything, then reply with exactly the word: done
